title: How multikey indexes on arrays behave
description: Indexing an array field creates a multikey index with one index entry per element, which constrains compound use.
body: Reference for indexing array-valued fields. When an indexed field holds an array, MongoDB builds a multikey index with a separate entry per element, so a query matching any element can use it. Use when a document embeds an array you filter on. Do not put two array fields in the same compound index; MongoDB cannot build a compound multikey index over more than one array field and will reject it. Validation signals include explain reporting isMultiKey true and the query served by an IXSCAN. Risk boundary: multikey indexes can balloon when arrays are large, so bound array length and confirm index size stays reasonable before relying on element queries.
